items.title: Why Premier League clubs keep signing each other's players for the same fee
items.summary: Premier League clubs are doing business with each other at an unusually higher rate this season.

items.title: Why clamor for Man United, Tottenham 'DNA' makes no sense
items.summary: Fans of Man United and Spurs expect a certain style of play. Nobody complains when their team is winning.

items.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
items.summary: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

items.title: Saved by 'special talent' - but did penalty award wrong Brighton?
items.summary: Charalampos Kostoulas earned Brighton a point with a stunning overhead kick but were the Seagulls right to be angry over an earlier penalty award?

items.title: Monaco's Balogun: Playing Real Madrid 'a dream'
items.summary: United States men's national team and Monaco striker Folarin Balogun told ESPN he will achieve one of his dreams on Tuesday when the Ligue 1 club face Real Madrid at the Bernabéu in the Champions League.

items.title: Source: Sporting KC majority stake sold at $700M
items.summary: A minority owner in MLS side Sporting Kansas City has completed a deal to purchase a majority stake in the club from its current owners, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: City confirm $26.8M Guehi signing from Palace
items.summary: Manchester City have announced the signing of England centre-back Marc Guéhi from Crystal Palace on a five-and-a-half-year deal.

items.title: Guardiola verheugd door komst Guéhi: 'Hij kan hier jaren spelen'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola is in zijn nopjes met de komst van Marc Guéhi. Wat de Spaanse trainer betreft, maakt de Engelse verdediger jaren deel uit van Manchester City. Guéhi kwam maandag over van Crystal Palace.

items.title: Assist Van Hecke op wondergoal redt punt voor Brighton na discutabele penalty
items.summary: Het Premier League-duel tussen Brighton & Hove Albion en Bournemouth heeft maandagavond geen winnaar opgeleverd. Een heerlijke omhaal van Charalampos Kostoulas redde in blessuretijd een punt voor de thuisploeg.

items.title: Transfers PSV: Schaars bevestigt aanstaande transfer
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij PSV op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Eindhovenaren, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: PSV verkoopt jonge aanvaller aan Levante
items.summary: Tai Abed neemt na vierenhalf jaar afscheid van PSV. De 21-jarige Israëliër zet zijn loopbaan voort bij Levante, de nummer negentien van La Liga.

items.title: Kostoulas’s brilliant bicycle kick rescues point for Brighton against Bournemouth
items.summary: Just when it seemed that another match would be dominated by the dreariness of a debatable video assistant referee decision, a moment of majesty from Charalampos Kostoulas provided a pertinent reminder of the beauty that football can provide. With his side staring at what would have been a controversial defeat, the 18-year-old Kostoulas found himself facing away from the Bournemouth goal near the penalty spot when the ball bounced towards him in the first minute of injury time. A touch on his chest bought time to set himself, before a wonderful bicycle kick sent the Amex Stadium wild. Continue reading...

items.title: Ibrahimovic junior: 'Ik speel bij Ajax omdat ik mezelf ben, niet door iets anders'
items.summary: Maximilian Ibrahimovic maakte maandagavond zijn eerste speelminuten in een Ajax-shirt, met een invalbeurt bij de beloftenploeg tegen Roda JC Kerkrade (2-2). Op vergelijkingen met zijn vader zat de jonge Zweed na afloop duidelijk niet te wachten.

items.title: Last-gasp overhead kick and VAR controversy as Brighton nick dramatic draw
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Brighton’s match against Bournemouth in the Premier League.

items.title: ‘A late moment of magic!’ | Kostoulas scores sensational 90th-minute overhead kick!
items.summary: Kostoulas scores sensational 90th minute overhead kick for Brighton in their Premier League game against Bournemouth.

items.title: Man Utd's complicated relationship with Class of 92
items.summary: Manchester United and the class of 92 have a complicated relationship. But is it a problem?

items.title: ‘We are getting closer’: Gabriel Jesus targets trophies and staying at Arsenal
items.summary: Brazilian striker yet to hold talks over new deal Arteta keen to sustain Champions League form at Inter Gabriel Jesus wants to fulfil his ambition of winning trophies with Arsenal and believes they are “getting closer” after three successive runners-up finishes in the Premier League. Mikel Arteta’s side head into their meeting with Inter in Milan on Tuesday night top of the Champions League table after winning all six of their games, while Arsenal also have a seven‑point lead in the Premier League and remain in both domestic cup competitions. Jesus is the only member of Arsenal’s squad who has won the title having picked up four winners’ medals during his time at Manchester City. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Grow up!' - Scholes and Butt continue war of words with Martinez
items.summary: Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt have continued their war of words with Manchester United's Lisandro Martinez, with Butt telling the Argentine to "grow up" over his response to criticism.

items.title: Brahim Díaz learns cruel Panenka lesson to break Morocco’s hearts in Afcon final
items.summary: Misjudged penalty after Senegal’s walk-off chaos leaves forward facing a lifetime of criticism After Portugal had beaten England in the World Cup quarter-final in 2006, Cristiano Ronaldo was asked how he had looked so calm taking his penalty in the shootout when England’s players appeared crushed by the occasion. For a moment he seemed baffled by the question, then he explained that those moments are what he lives for. Where others feel pressure, he sees opportunity. What, you wonder, did Brahim Díaz see during the Afcon final on Sunday? When his shoulder was tugged by El Hadji Malick Diouf and he collapsed, did he consider the consequences? When he howled in the face of the Democratic Republic of the Congo referee Jean-Jacques Ndala Ngambo as he waited for the verdict of the video assistant referee, did it occur to him he would take the penalty if it were given? He had scored one against Mali in the group stage, but that was with Achraf Hakimi, a very fine penalty taker, off the pitch. Continue reading...

items.title: Thomas Frank insists ‘everything normal’ despite turmoil at Tottenham
items.summary: Manager has ‘been feeling the trust’ from club hierarchy Spurs hampered by injury crisis before visit of Dortmund Thomas Frank has insisted the Tottenham hierarchy are standing with him in the face of the storm gripping the club. The manager’s job is in the balance, his situation precarious after the home defeat against West Ham on Saturday. The Spurs support were so incensed by the result and the continuation of the team’s terrible Premier League form – they have won twice in their past 13 league matches – that they demanded Frank be “sacked in the morning”. Continue reading...
